sidebar.hideProjectsWithoutActiveTasksDescriptio: Filters the sidebar to projects with at least one visible, non-archived task, keeping project navigation focused on current work.
sidebar.expressModeDescription: When enabled, clicking the “+” on a project creates a task instantly using defaults (default branch + last-used agent), skipping the home configuration panel. Run the home flow once first so the defaults are populated.
sidebar.copyProjectPathFailed: The project path could not be copied to the clipboard.

renameProject.hint: Leave empty to use the default name ({{name}}).

tasks.autoGenerateNameDescription: Automatically suggests a task name when creating a new task.

tasks.enableTmuxDescription: Run agent sessions and terminals in tmux sessions by default.
tasks.preArchiveCommandDescription: When set, archiving a task first sends this text to the task's last-used conversation and waits for the agent to finish. Leave empty to archive immediately.

terminal.fontDescription: Choose the font family for the terminal.
terminal.autoCopySelectedDescription: Automatically copy text to clipboard when you select it in the terminal.

defaultAgent.title: Default agent
defaultAgent.description: The agent that will be selected by default when creating a new task.

settings.serverUnavailable: {{product}} server is currently unavailable. Please try again later.

keyboard.shortcutRemovedDescription: {{label}} no longer has a key binding.
keyboard.shortcutConflictDescription: Conflicts with "{{label}}". Choose a different shortcut.

customCommand.cliCommandTooltip: The CLI command to execute (e.g., claude, codex)
customCommand.commandPrefixTooltip: Prefix for agent-native commands and skills. Codex uses $, Claude uses /.
customCommand.resumeFlagTooltip: Flag used when resuming a session (e.g., -c -r)
customCommand.extraArgsTooltip: Extra flags appended to the command (e.g. --enable-all-github-mcp-tools)
customCommand.envVarsTooltip: Environment variables set when running the agent
customCommand.initialPromptFlagTooltip: Flag for passing initial prompt (empty means pass directly)

branchPrefix.autoPushDescription: Push the new branch to the selected project remote and set upstream after creation.
branchPrefix.autoUpdateGitignoreDescription: When Yoda writes CLI hook configs, also add their paths to .gitignore.

openInApps.tooltipNotInstalled: Install this tool to show it in menu

onboarding.signInDescription: Yoda uses your Lovstudio account for sync and personalization. You can connect GitHub separately.

import.headerSingleDescription: Found {{projectsCount}} and {{tasksCount}} from your previous Yoda installation
import.headerMultiTitle: Do you want to import projects and tasks from other Yoda versions?
import.conflictsDescription: These projects exist in both selected versions. Choose which version to keep.

home.headline: What's next?
home.promptPlaceholder: Ask yoda anything. Type / or $ to use skills, or @ to mention files.
home.voiceTooltip: Trigger the Typeless hands-free voice shortcut
home.voiceTriggerFailedToast: Could not trigger Typeless voice input
home.modeCompareDesc: Start several agents on isolated branches.
home.modeReviewDesc: Implementation and reviewer agents loop on the same branch.
home.modeTeamDesc: CEO agent decomposes work for specialist agents.
home.reviewRoundLimit: Review loop stops after {{count}} rounds if issues remain.
home.agentSystemPromptPlaceholder: Describe this agent's role, constraints, and operating style.
home.needProjectHint: Pick a project to send a message.
home.strategyAria: Choose how to start the task
home.strategyNoWorktreeDesc: Fastest path. Changes land on the current branch in place — no isolation. Best for tiny fixes or solo work.
home.strategyNewBranchDesc: Spins up an isolated copy on a new branch. Keeps {{branch}} clean and lets multiple tasks run in parallel.
home.reviewStrategySameBranchDesc: A and B share the current branch. Best when the loop should directly refine the working tree.
home.reviewStrategyNewBranchDesc: A and B share one isolated review branch while keeping {{branch}} clean.

skills.subtitle: Extend your agents with reusable skill modules
skills.catalogDescription: Skills from the <openai>OpenAI</openai> and <anthropic>Anthropic</anthropic> catalogs. Install a skill to make it available across all your coding agents. Skills follow the open <standard>Agent Skills</standard> standard. If you want to use skills from another library, feel free to let us know through the feedback modal.

merge.label: Merge pull request

squash.label: Squash and merge
squash.description: All commits from this branch will be combined into one commit in the base branch.

rebase.description: All commits from this branch will be rebased and added to the base branch.
